definitions.text: To move or be moved into something. To descend or submerge (or to cause to do so) into a liquid or similar substance.
examples: A stone sinks in water. The sun gradually sank in the west.
An iceberg sank the Titanic. British battleships sank the Bismarck.

definitions.text: To descend or submerge (or to cause to do so) into a liquid or similar substance.
examples: A stone sinks in water. The sun gradually sank in the west.

definitions.text: A basin used for holding water for washing.
A drain for carrying off wastewater.
